title: Elementary storage supervisors
description: Schedule and dispatch workers, work crews, equipment, or service vehicles for conveyance of materials, freight, or passengers, or for normal installation, service, or emergency repairs rendered outside the place of business. Duties may include using radio, telephone, or computer to transmit assignments and compiling statistics and reports on work progress. Verify and maintain records on incoming and outgoing shipments involving inventory. Duties include verifying and recording incoming merchandise or material and arranging for the transportation of products. May prepare items for shipment. Weigh, measure, and check materials, supplies, and equipment for the purpose of keeping relevant records. Duties are primarily clerical by nature. Includes workers who collect and keep record of samples of products or materials. (Based on 10 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Schedule or dispatch workers, work crews, equipment, or service vehicles to appropriate locations according to customer requests or needs, using radios or telephones. Monitor personnel or equipment locations and utilization to coordinate service and schedules. Prepare daily work and run schedules. Record and maintain files or records of customer requests, work performed, charges, expenses, and inventory information. Relay work orders and messages to or from work crews, supervisors, or inspectors. Confer with customers or supervising personnel to address questions or service requests. Determine types or amounts of equipment, materials, or personnel required according to work orders. Maintain, monitor, and clean work areas to ensure compliance with safety regulations. Ensure a safe working environment by monitoring safety procedures and equipment.
Record numbers of units handled or moved, using daily production sheets or work tickets, and maintain records on incoming and outgoing shipments involving inventory.
Read work orders or receive oral instructions to determine work assignments or material or equipment needs.
Carry needed tools or supplies from storage or trucks and return them after use.
Operate forklifts, pallet jacks, power lifts, or front-end loaders to load items onto trucks for shipping.
Sort materials, such as metals, glass, wood, paper, or plastics, into appropriate containers for recycling, and prepare items for shipment.
Clean recycling yard by sweeping, raking, picking up broken glass and loose paper debris, or moving barrels and bins, and inspect or lubricate recyclable collection equipment.
Attach identifying tags to containers or mark them with identifying information.
Sort metals to separate high-grade metals, such as copper, brass, and aluminum, for recycling.
Collect recyclable materials and deposit them into designated facilities or onto conveyor belts.
Record logs of recycled materials or waste chemicals removed from products.
